intent: make the export command's failure messages helpful when the output path is bad

expectations.text: when the output path does not exist, the error tells the user what was wrong and what to do
expectations.reason: 'helpful / actionable' is a judgment about wording

producer_result.captured_verbatim:
Error: cannot write to '/nonexistent/dir' — the directory does not exist.
  Try: mkdir -p /nonexistent/dir, or pass an existing --out path.
exit=2

expected_guard: trail written only when the gate ran (DDD6/ADR-009); off-ramp writes none
